plainEnglish: At Level AAA, warn users up front how long inactivity can last before they lose data—unless you preserve their data for more than 20 hours. People may step away mid-task, and they need to know whether their work will survive.
whyItMatters: Users who need to pause a task—for a break or due to a disability—lose unsaved data without warning if they don't know the inactivity limit.
quickCheck: Confirm the app warns users about the inactivity-timeout duration up front, or that data is preserved for 20+ hours.
commonMistakes: Setting an inactivity data-loss timeout with no advance warning of its duration.
Assuming users know how long they can be idle before losing work.
Not preserving in-progress data across the timeout, so idle users lose everything.

codeExamples.label: Warn about the inactivity limit up front
codeExamples.code: For your security, you'll be signed out after 15 minutes of inactivity. Unsaved changes will be lost.

codeExamples.label: Silent data loss after idle time
codeExamples.code:
// Clears the draft after idle time with no prior warning
setTimeout(() => clearDraft(), 15 * 60 * 1000);
